formulas.name: Depreciation Formula
formulas.variables: W = Depreciation per annum, C = Cost of asset, A = Annuity for the life of asset, N = Number of years
formulas.notes: This formula is used to calculate depreciation per annum.

formulas.name: Reducing Balance Method
formulas.variables: W = Depreciation per annum, C = Cost of asset, r = Rate of depreciation, n = Number of years
formulas.notes: This method is used to calculate depreciation where asset value decreases by a fixed percentage every year.

formulas.name: Straight Line Method
formulas.variables: W = Depreciation per annum, C = Cost of asset, n = Number of years
formulas.notes: This method is used to calculate depreciation where asset value decreases by an equal amount every year.

formulas.name: Provisions Formula
formulas.variables: Estimated Loss = Estimated amount of loss, Accumulated Loss = Accumulated amount of loss
formulas.notes: This formula is used to calculate provisions for estimated and accumulated losses.

formulas.name: Reserves Formula
formulas.variables: Profit = Net profit of the company, Dividend = Dividend declared by the company
formulas.notes: This formula is used to calculate reserves by subtracting dividend from net profit.

formulas.name: Statement of Accounting Treatment for Depreciation
formulas.formula: Depreciation is an expense, not a liability.
formulas.notes: This statement emphasizes that depreciation is an expense, not a liability, and is deducted from profit to arrive at net profit.

formulas.name: Statement of Accounting Treatment for Provisions
formulas.formula: Provisions are recorded as liabilities and expenses in the same period.
formulas.notes: This statement emphasizes that provisions are recorded as both liabilities and expenses in the same period.

formulas.name: Statement of Accounting Treatment for Reserves
formulas.notes: This statement emphasizes that reserves are recorded as equity, not liabilities or expenses.
